title: Add bidirectional arrow, text rotation, free transform tool, and update icon styles in attack path visualizer

requirements.summary: Add bidirectional vertical arrow icon to attack path visualizer toolbar as draggable element
acceptanceCriteria: A bidirectional vertical arrow icon appears in the AttackPathToolbar
The arrow icon can be dragged from the toolbar and placed onto the canvas
The placed arrow icon can be repositioned like other icons
The arrow icon maintains consistent styling with other attack path elements

file_operations.description: Add a new bidirectional vertical arrow icon to the toolbar's draggable icons array. Include the icon with type 'bidirectional-arrow' alongside existing icons like 'attacker', 'computer', etc. Ensure the icon is draggable using the same onDragStart mechanism as other toolbar icons.

Add rendering logic for the 'bidirectional-arrow' icon type. Create an SVG-based bidirectional vertical arrow (up and down parallel arrows) that matches the styling of existing attack path elements. Ensure the icon maintains draggability and interaction consistency with other icon types.
Ensure the canvas correctly handles drop and placement of the new 'bidirectional-arrow' icon type, treating it like other draggable icons with full repositioning support.

requirements.summary: Enable text label rotation in attack path visualizer
acceptanceCriteria: Text labels can be rotated using a rotation control or handle
Rotation angle is persisted in the text label state
Rotated text labels maintain their rotation when saved and loaded
Rotation is smooth and intuitive to use

file_operations.description: Add a 'rotation' property (number, default 0) to the TextLabel type in the state management hook. Update state setters to handle rotation changes for individual text labels.

Add rotation control UI for selected text labels. Implement a rotation handle (e.g., circular handle above the text label) that allows users to rotate text by dragging. Apply CSS transform rotation to text labels based on their rotation state. Ensure rotation persists when text is moved or edited.
Update the diagram state serialization to include the rotation property when converting text labels to backend DiagramState format. Ensure rotation values are preserved during save operations.
Update the diagram state deserialization logic to extract and apply the rotation property when loading text labels from the backend DiagramState format into frontend state.

requirements.summary: Implement free transform tool for moving and resizing icons and text labels
acceptanceCriteria: A free transform tool is available in the toolbar
When active, selected icons display resize handles at corners/edges
When active, selected text labels display resize handles
Icons and text labels can be resized by dragging handles
Icons and text labels can be moved while in transform mode
Resized dimensions are persisted in element state
Transform handles are visible and easy to interact with

file_operations.description: Add a 'Free Transform' tool button to the toolbar alongside existing drawing tools (freehand, line, arrow, text). The button should toggle the transform mode and provide visual feedback when active.

Add 'width' and 'height' properties to Icon and TextLabel types. Add a new state property 'activeTransformTool' (boolean) to track when free transform mode is active. Add a 'selectedElementId' state to track which element is currently selected for transformation.

Implement free transform functionality: When free transform mode is active, display resize handles (corner and edge handles) around the selected icon or text label. Implement drag handlers for resize handles that update element width/height. Allow moving elements while in transform mode. Ensure all transform changes persist in state.
Update icon rendering to support custom width and height properties from state. Apply dimensions using CSS or SVG attributes while maintaining aspect ratio or allowing free resize as needed.
Update diagram state serialization to include width and height properties for icons and text labels when saving to backend.
Update diagram state deserialization to extract and apply width and height properties when loading icons and text labels from backend.

requirements.summary: Change .exe icon to text-based representation displaying '.exe'
acceptanceCriteria: The .exe icon in the toolbar displays as text reading '.exe'
When placed on the canvas, the .exe element renders as text '.exe'
The text-based .exe element maintains consistent styling with other elements
The .exe element remains draggable and functional

file_operations.description: Update the rendering logic for the 'exe' icon type to display as text '.exe' instead of using the image asset frontend/public/assets/generated/exe-icon.dim_64x64.png. Apply consistent text styling (font, color, background) that matches other text-based elements in the attack path visualizer.
Update the toolbar representation of the .exe icon to display as text '.exe' instead of the image icon. Ensure the text representation is clearly visible and maintains the draggable behavior.

requirements.summary: Change Excel icon to display 'Microsoft Excel' text instead of graphical icon
acceptanceCriteria: The Excel icon in the toolbar displays as text reading 'Microsoft Excel'
When placed on the canvas, the Excel element renders as text 'Microsoft Excel'
The text-based Excel element maintains consistent styling
The Excel element remains draggable and functional

file_operations.description: Update the rendering logic for the Excel icon type to display as text 'Microsoft Excel' instead of using the image asset frontend/public/assets/generated/excel-icon.dim_64x64.png. Apply consistent text styling that matches other text-based elements.
Update the toolbar representation of the Excel icon to display as text 'Microsoft Excel' instead of the image icon. Ensure the text is readable and maintains draggable behavior.

requirements.summary: Change Word icon to display 'Microsoft Word' text instead of graphical icon
acceptanceCriteria: The Word icon in the toolbar displays as text reading 'Microsoft Word'
When placed on the canvas, the Word element renders as text 'Microsoft Word'
The text-based Word element maintains consistent styling
The Word element remains draggable and functional

file_operations.description: Update the rendering logic for the Word icon type to display as text 'Microsoft Word' instead of using the image asset frontend/public/assets/generated/word-icon.dim_64x64.png. Apply consistent text styling that matches other text-based elements.
Update the toolbar representation of the Word icon to display as text 'Microsoft Word' instead of the image icon. Ensure the text is readable and maintains draggable behavior.

requirements.summary: Remove IP Address icon from attack path visualizer toolbar
acceptanceCriteria: The IP Address icon is not visible in the AttackPathToolbar
Existing diagrams with IP Address icons remain unaffected
The IP Address icon type is removed from the available icon types for new placements

file_operations.description: Remove the IP Address icon from the toolbar's draggable icons array. Ensure that any references to 'ip' or 'ip-address' icon type are removed from the toolbar UI, preventing users from adding new IP Address icons to the canvas.
